originalTitle: Wall Street ends sharply lower as jobs data fuels rate hike fears
summary: STORY: Wall Street’s nine-week winning streak came to an abrupt halt on Friday, with the Dow dropping about one-and-a-third percent, the S&P 500 sliding more than 2.6% and the tech-heavy Nasdaq tumbling nearly 4.2%, its largest one-day percentage loss in more than a year. Selling was concentrated among chip stocks and other technology favorites that have surged in recent weeks as the Nasdaq and S&P 500 rose repeatedly to fresh highs. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index erased more than $1 trillion in stock market value on Friday, suffering its largest one-day percentage plunge since March of 2020. Eric Lynch, managing director and co-portfolio manager of Suncoast Equity Management, said tech stocks tanked due to rate-hike and AI spending concerns. "The first factor is the blowout jobs report this morning. That has taken the chances of another Fed cut off the table for 2026. That hits long-duration, most exciting speculative stocks the most. [FLASH] But also there's been grumblings about AI, just some concerns about whether or not the token usage is really translating to great returns. And you've seen some consternation kind of bubbling up." Among individual movers, AI chipmaker Nvidia, the largest company by market value, lost more than 6%, while Intel, Micron, AMD and Broadcom all slid between about 8% and 13.5%. Shares of Lululemon Athletica slumped about 8.5% after the athletic apparel maker cut its annual profit forecast and projected second-quarter earnings well below Wall Street estimates. And a more than 4% drop in bitcoin hit cryptocurrency firms, with shares of Coinbase and Strategy each tumbling about 7%.

[FLASH] But also there's been grumblings about AI, just some concerns about whether or not the token usage is really translating to great returns. And you've seen some consternation kind of bubbling up." Among individual movers, AI chipmaker Nvidia, the largest company by market value, lost more than 6%, while Intel, Micron, AMD and Broadcom all slid between about 8% and 13.5%. chineseTitle: 就业数据加剧加息担忧，华尔街收跌
chineseSummary: 故事：华尔街九周的连涨势头在周五突然停止，道指下跌约三分之一，标准普尔 500 指数下跌超过 2.6%，以科技股为主的纳斯达克指数下跌近 4.2%，创一年多来最大单日跌幅。随着纳斯达克和标准普尔 500 指数屡屡创出新高，芯片股和其他热门科技股最近几周大幅上涨，抛售主要集中在芯片股和其他科技股上。周五，费城半导体指数蒸发了超过 1 万亿美元的股票市值，遭遇 2020 年 3 月以来最大单日百分比跌幅。Suncoast Equity Management 董事总经理兼联席投资组合经理埃里克·林奇 (Eric Lynch) 表示，由于加息和人工智能支出担忧，科技股暴跌。 “第一个因素是今天早上井喷式的就业报告。这冒着美联储再次削减 2026 年利率的可能性。这对长期、最令人兴奋的投机股票打击最大。[快闪]但也有人对人工智能有抱怨，只是担心代币的使用是否真的能转化为巨大的回报。你已经看到了一些惊愕的情绪。”在个股中，市值最大的人工智能芯片制造商英伟达(Nvidia)下跌超过6%，而英特尔(Intel)、美光(Micron)、AMD和博通(Broadcom)的跌幅均在约8%至13.5%之间。在运动服装制造商 Lululemon Athletica 下调年度利润预期并预计第二季度盈利远低于华尔街预期后，该公司股价下跌约 8.5%。比特币下跌超过 4%，对加密货币公司造成了打击，Coinbase 和 Strategy 的股价均下跌约 7%。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
